title: Add Python TUI automated tests and terminal snapshots
description: Why this exists: terminal UIs regress easily in layout, key handling, and transport state transitions. What needs to be done: add unit tests for state/model logic, fake BLE and fake CoAP transports, view-model tests, key-flow tests, and terminal snapshot or render assertions for core screens. Acceptance criteria: tests run on developer macOS without BLE hardware; Linux tests can use BlueZ mocks or fakes without requiring adapter access; snapshots cover connection picker, inbox, compose, status, mesh, config, logs, and diagnostics; CI instructions or skip rules are documented for hardware-only tests.
notes:
Implementation evidence for TUI automated tests and terminal snapshots:
- Added assertion-based terminal SVG snapshot coverage for native TUI connection/disconnected state, inbox, compose, status, nodes, mesh, config, logs, and diagnostics screens using fake client state. No generated screenshot files are committed.
- Added focus cleanup when leaving Chats so compose inputs are synced and disabled before keyboard navigation reaches other screens; this prevents focused inputs from swallowing subsequent screen navigation in snapshot/key-flow tests.
- Documented no-hardware native TUI/client test workflow in python/README.md, including BLE fake requirements and explicit hardware marker/environment-gate expectations for any future real adapter/board tests.
- Created follow-up project-LICHEN-xmrv for a real native TUI connection picker flow; current code has transport state but no picker screen to snapshot.

Local validation:
- cd python && uv run ruff check src/lichen/tui tests/tui pyproject.toml -> passed.
- cd python && uv run mypy src/lichen/tui tests/tui -> passed, no issues in 4 source files.
- cd python && uv run pytest tests/tui tests/client -q -> 74 passed in 8.42s.
- git diff --check -> passed.

Code review:
- Bernoulli review: no blocking P0/P1/P2 findings; verified connection picker gap is tracked by project-LICHEN-xmrv and no-hardware docs are appropriate.
- Fermat review: no P0/P1/P2 findings; snapshot test is fake-backed/no-hardware, README guidance matches CI path, and focus/input sync is targeted.

No EC2 used for this Python-only task.
